articleTitle: Effectiveness of mHealth-Based Nutritional Interventions on Iron Status of Pregnant Women: Systematic Review of Randomized Controlled Trials.
takeaways: Mobile health (mHealth) nutritional interventions show promise as effective alternatives to traditional healthcare delivery for improving iron status in pregnant women, particularly in areas with limited healthcare access

Iron deficiency anemia disproportionately affects pregnant women in low-resource regions, where traditional interventions face implementation barriers including poor supplementation adherence and inadequate nutrition counseling
mHealth-based approaches can address key implementation challenges by overcoming healthcare access limitations and filling gaps in nutrition knowledge that hinder effective anemia prevention
The systematic review of randomized controlled trials demonstrates that mobile health platforms can optimize hemoglobin levels in pregnant women through targeted nutritional interventions
